slides.title: Introduction to CNNs
slides.content: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) are a class of deep learning models primarily used for analyzing visual data. They have been successfully applied to tasks like image and video recognition, image classification, and more.

slides.title: How CNNs Work
slides.content: CNNs are composed of layers that automatically and adaptively learn spatial hierarchies of features from input images. They typically include convolutional layers, pooling layers, and fully connected layers.

slides.title: Applications of CNNs
slides.content:
CNNs are widely used in various applications: 
1. Object Detection 
2. Semantic Segmentation 
3. Image Classification 
4. Medical Image Analysis

slides.title: Example: Image Classification
slides.content: In image classification, CNNs are used to categorize images into predefined classes. An example of this is the classification of handwritten digits using the MNIST dataset.

slides.title: Key Components of CNNs
slides.content:
1. Convolutional Layers: Perform convolution operations to extract features. 
2. Pooling Layers: Reduce dimensionality. 
3. Fully Connected Layers: Perform classification tasks.

slides.title: Code Example: Simple CNN with Python
slides.content: Here is a basic structure of a CNN using Python and popular deep learning libraries like TensorFlow or PyTorch.

visual_assets.code:
def simple_cnn():
    model = Sequential()
    model.add(Conv2D(32, (3, 3), activation='relu', input_shape=(64, 64, 3)))
    model.add(MaxPooling2D((2, 2)))
    model.add(Flatten())
    model.add(Dense(64, activation='relu'))
    model.add(Dense(10, activation='softmax'))
    return model
